title: 30/04/2026 - [F&E Alignment] Scope Walkthrough: Fixed Fee / Contract Modification
report_link: [F&E Alignment] Scope Walkthrough: Fixed Fee / Contract Modification
summary:
The meeting reviewed a requested change to limit case approval and rejection actions to designated queue members and to determine which approval workflow the restriction should affect. Attendees clarified that the change applies specifically to the Kmod approval process version 4, covering contract modification and six-fee SOW workflows for T4C, and not to other approval processes. The group examined source links and reference documents to reconcile a business-provided requirement with a "cheat sheet" backlog entry that appeared inconsistent.
Participants agreed to demonstrate the updates to a developer and QA via a short walkthrough involving Dana, Anu, Yumi, and a BT-team member to prevent implementation errors, and to explore transferring work already completed in a non-UAD environment into UAD if feasible. Drashti committed to investigate the cheat-sheet entry and provide the business document link, and Kilton committed to schedule the walkthrough meeting either the following morning or Monday depending on Dana’s availability. Open items remain the cheat-sheet reconciliation and the environment transfer feasibility.

action_items.text: Drashti will send the business document link to Kilton (06:33)
Kilton will set up a walkthrough call to walk the assignee through the updates (Kilton, Dana, Anu, Yumi, and a BT-team member) (10:32)
Drashti will investigate the cheat-sheet requirement entry and determine what is missing or incorrect (11:09)
Kilton will check Dana’s availability and schedule the walkthrough for tomorrow morning if Dana is working, otherwise for Monday morning (12:40)
Kilton will add a BT-team member who created the original TD to the walkthrough meeting (13:03)

key_questions: Should the restriction be applied to all approval processes or only a specific one? (03:32)
Can you send the specific business document that contains the requirement? (06:26)
blockers: Participants agreed to demonstrate the updates to a developer and QA via a short walkthrough involving Dana, Anu, Yumi, and a BT-team member to prevent implementation errors, and to explore transferring work already completed in a non-UAD environment into UAD if feasible. Drashti committed to investigate the cheat-sheet entry and provide the business document link, and Kilton committed to schedule the walkthrough meeting either the following morning or Monday depending on Dana’s availability. Open items remain the cheat-sheet reconciliation and the environment transfer feasibility.

title: 30/04/2026 - Linear Basics: AMER/EMEA - Option 2
summary:
The meeting oriented DET and customer success teams on how Linear will be adopted for intake, triage, planning, and engineering workflows. It reviewed Linear’s hierarchy (initiatives, projects, issues) and mapped existing GUS concepts into Linear (program→initiative, EPIC/project→project, work items→issues), described label groups and preconfigured fields, and demonstrated the Linear UI, agent, and fast command workflow.
The team examined Slack integrations and triage: two Slack apps for unstructured threads and structured asks, templates enforcing required fields, automated triage rules, triage intelligence and agent automations that request more information or route items, and acceptance/decline/duplicate responses back to reporters. Project and issue workspace features were covered, including saved list/board views, project timeline/Gantt with health and milestone metadata, the prediction feature (with a caution about loose scoping), and a burn-up example (83 estimate points; 34 started; 10 completed). Cycles are configured as two-week sprints with capacity based on the last three cycles, immutable start dates after launch, and Insights dashboards for lead/cycle time and SLAs. The meeting closed with onboarding logistics: Wave 1 materials, trainings (Lara scheduled by end of day or next morning), a follow-up with Kathy about shared services UX, the PDO and champion model, and available playbooks and office hours.

action_items.text: The team will configure two-week cycles at the pillar level in advance of onboarding new members. (38:46)
Meeting organizers will schedule shared-services trainings for Lara by end of day or early morning tomorrow (53:36)
Meeting organizers will schedule a follow-up meeting with Kathy to discuss shared services UX (53:41)
Sarah will demo specific flows in Linear when requested in the channel (54:12)

Meeting participants will review the DUT Linear playbook for configuration and workflow guidance. (01:17:34)

title: 30/04/2026 - DSU AMER
report_link: Finance Repo Access and UAT Coordination
summary:
The meeting focused on progress and blockers across the finance commission repository, integration research, testing, and UAT coordination. The team confirmed a support ticket has been raised to obtain DevOps access so Participant 6 can begin scratch-arc work once approved; Participant 6 will in the meantime add missing fields and dependencies to the finance repo and has posted errors to the channel. A shared-access request referenced by Participant 3 will be raised by Participant 1. Document updates and Basecamp integration research were reviewed, with Participant 5 attaching documents, flagging Canvas-link questions for Slavic, and planning a follow-up with Anita on Monday. GOV cloud approval flow and PSR deletion test cases were tested and approved with no blockers from Participant 8.
Development work discussed included a functional Apex trigger fix but missing trigger registry and handler in repos, with Participant 2 completing test classes and deciding on registry inclusion after further analysis by Monday. The team reviewed several development stories and UAT readiness: one story awaits Venkat and QA confirmation, another is QA-tested but needs UAT user alignment for release tagging. A static record-type check is being converted to custom metadata to support non-omni record types, Jenkins successfully deployed a shared metadata change, and permission scenarios for SLAG security on legal child cases remain under analysis. The group also clarified UAT access tasks, agreed to verify SSO/login for business users, assign extra forms when needed, and identified volunteers to help onboard or coordinate UAT testers such as Joseph and Jessica if access issues occur.

action_items.text: Participant 6 will add the missing fields and dependencies to the finance repo to resolve the posted errors. (01:27)
Participant 6 will start scratch-arc work once DevOps access approval is granted. (01:44)
Participant 1 will raise the shared-access request needed to create scratch arcs as described by Participant 3. (02:24)
Participant 5 will share the attached documents on Chatter for team review. (04:04)
Participant 5 will check remaining queries with Anita on Monday regarding items that are not working. (04:16)
Participant 2 will complete the test classes for the functional fix before starting trigger-registry work. (06:17)
Participant 8 will increase the story points to five for the case-related work. (07:13)
Participant 2 will analyze trigger-registry and trigger-handler configuration and provide an update on Monday about whether it will be handled separately or included in the story. (08:37)
Participant 4 will double check and confirm the UAT user assignments and releases (13:06)
Participant 1 will trigger the QA run for the deployed shared metadata change (18:35)
Participant 1 will start work on the other related story next week (18:55)
Meeting participant will run the access and process questions by Venkat and Bellary to confirm past sprint practice (19:59)
Participant 4 will document the process for moving forward with user access and release handling once Venkat and Valerie define it (20:39)
Participant 1 will cross-check Joseph Wacker's recent login history and SSO status for UAT access (20:59)
Participant 4 will coordinate a UAT test with Joseph and Jessica if notified that they need onboarding help (23:22)

No one touches the trigger handler, right? (11:40)
When are we targeting this as part of which release? (15:08)
Do you have access, Lakshmi, in UAT? (21:25)

title: 30/04/2026 - Daily
summary: A gravação é um breve encontro diário para tratar de uma pendência de homologação relacionada ao ambiente "parcial" e ao fluxo de testes com QA e perfis de usuário, com referência a apoio da Adri para validação e à necessidade de comunicação da review na segunda-feira. O grupo concordou em executar testes em parcial, gravar os resultados e validar perfis para garantir que o que foi desenvolvido em QA funcione em parcial antes da review; também houve menção a disponibilidade de agenda para focar nessas atividades hoje e a expectativa de que Jarine comunique a data de review para garantir participação.

action_items.text: Participant 2: Homologar o que for necessário junto com a Adri e dar direcionamento para os testes em parcial. (00:33)
Jarine: Comunicar formalmente que a review será na segunda-feira para cobrar a participação de todos. (01:06)
Adri: Fazer double check dos testes gravados em parcial e validar os perfis que deveriam funcionar. (01:29)
Participant 2: Executar e gravar os testes em parcial usando os perfis adequados. (01:29)

A gravação é um breve encontro diário para tratar de uma pendência de homologação relacionada ao ambiente "parcial" e ao fluxo de testes com QA e perfis de usuário, com referência a apoio da Adri para validação e à necessidade de comunicação da review na segunda-feira. O grupo concordou em executar testes em parcial, gravar os resultados e validar perfis para garantir que o que foi desenvolvido em QA funcione em parcial antes da review; também houve menção a disponibilidade de agenda para focar nessas atividades hoje e a expectativa de que Jarine comunique a data de review para garantir participação.

## 💬 Chapters & Topics

O início da conversa traz preocupações pessoais e uma menção específica a um problema com "Kyoto" que preocupa um participante. Há troca de comentários informais sobre estado e saúde que ocupam os primeiros momentos antes de entrarem no assunto técnico.

## Homologação, testes e responsabilidades (00:33)
Os participantes discutem o status da homologação, o papel da Adri em validar testes em QA e parcial, e a necessidade de direções claras para prosseguir com gravações e validações de perfis. title: 29/04/2026 - Chatter Deprecation long term with Matt
report_link: Chatter Deprecation long term with Matt
summary: The meeting reviewed the long-term plan for addressing duplicate chatter entries following the removal of Chatter from cases and the move to Slack for record-map channels. The team confirmed that button-related changes have already been coordinated with Sales Ops and are being aligned with the GIC team to determine the appropriate destination for buttons before any changes go live. Participant 2 raised the need to begin a broader discussion about the long-term solution and who should be involved to advance the effort. The conversation then shifted to existing automations that historically posted to Chatter and whether those automations need migrating to Slack; participants debated who should set the priority for that work. Participant 1 asked Participant 2 to schedule time with Jessica Berry and Venkat to review the automations and assess their importance, noting Participant 1 would be optional to attend and that if Jessica deems the automations unimportant the work would not proceed. Participant 2 agreed to initiate those discussions and to update Sinead and Kushal once the team decides on a plan.

action_items.text: Jessica Berry will confirm whether the automations that posted to Chatter are important to migrate to Slack (03:21)
Participant 2 will schedule time with Jessica Berry and Venkat to review automations that posted to Chatter and assess migration needs (03:28)
Participant 2 will start a discussion with Sinead and Jessica Berry about the remaining chatter duplication scope (03:45)
Participant 2 will inform Sinead and Kushal of the plan after aligning with Jessica and Venkat (04:32)

title: 29/04/2026 - NQE: Case Management Impacts
summary:
The meeting reviewed a planned change to move large forms onto a quote-related object and assessed downstream impacts on lookup fields, record types, and page layouts used in case management. The team confirmed that the Basecamp team likely does not need backend changes but should be notified if any fields require lookup eligibility adjustments, and agreed to clearly document Laura’s team’s required work and level of effort.
Participants noted uncertainty about knowledge articles and config forms, specifically whether updating the first action field will require republishing articles, and raised testing constraints because many knowledge setups are absent in lower environments. A proposed workaround for cases tied to legacy converted quotes would avoid changing lookups by using an unassociated form plus seller-operator follow-up. The group decided to wait for James’s updated data model before performing a full impact analysis, agreed to run an impact analysis in May, and confirmed a September release window with the build expected complete by mid/late July.

action_items.text: Participant 1 will serve as the point of contact to explain the MQE plan and facilitate impact-related changes for Laura’s team (03:52)
Participant 3 will start FIT on Monday, July 20th as planned (06:36)
Meeting participants will confirm any broad impacts that reference APTIS so the team has a full list of relevant fields, objects, and managed packages (06:52)
Participant 1 will share the code-based analysis and lookup analysis already completed with the team (07:19)
Participant 2 will perform an impact analysis in May (07:27)

title: 29/04/2026 - Entrega de livro e relato pessoal
report_link: Entrega de livro e relato pessoal
summary: A gravação registra Kilton falando sobre entregar um livro que escreveu e tentando falar com Paola. Kilton descreve seu histórico como escritora: escreveu há muitos anos, sentia medo de mostrar o trabalho à editora, recebeu feedback de que as histórias eram boas, e optou por não revelar o sucesso ao marido para evitar frustrações. Kilton afirma que o livro citado é o último que escreveu e contextualiza parte da história familiar: menciona Samuel como marido, a mudança para Inópolis, o nascimento dos filhos e que esperava o fim do pai antes de Samuel falecer.

title: 29/04/2026 - [VLI] [ Salesforce Day] Alinhamentos
summary:
A reunião teve como objetivo organizar a participação da VLI no OSF / Salesforce Day e alinhar logística, conteúdo e responsáveis para a apresentação Earth in Your Cloud. Foi confirmado que o evento ocorrerá em 12 de maio no escritório da VLI, com transporte via Uber Business ou reembolso de estacionamento; as passagens e translado de Kilton já foram aprovados.
Definiu-se cronograma e formato do material: primeira versão até 6 de maio com template a ser apresentado por Marcelino e apresentações curtas de cerca de 3–10 slides. O conteúdo previsto inclui Astori falando sobre o papel de PO e uso do Copilot com exemplos do case Moura, e Kilton apresentando o case Anima e Brevinho. Registrou-se sensibilidade interna em relação ao time operacional da VLI, com Mirna responsável por fornecer contexto adicional; Bruno substituirá o participante de férias na coordenação. A equipe confirmou disponibilidade e ofereceu apoio operacional à VLE, com um participante específico se oferecendo para ser acionado quando necessário. Também foram acertados planos logísticos e sociais relacionados à presença da equipe no local.

title: 29/04/2026 - One on One with Matt
summary:
The meeting reviewed current workload, the onboarding status of new contractors, and backlog readiness ahead of upcoming sprints. Participants reported that the newly onboarded contractors are competent and increasing throughput, which has raised pressure to produce clear requirements and unblock waiting stories for planning. One participant summarized detailed triage work already completed and noted a number of stories awaiting clarifications from Mohan and Valerie; they are actively contacting those stakeholders to resolve outstanding questions before the next grooming session.
To reduce friction and speed story writing, the team agreed to introduce a standardized Canvas requirements template and to migrate existing requirements into that format with the respective owners involved. They also decided to deprioritize agent-force stories in the near term so the owner can focus on F&E tickets, and to postpone the linear Wave 1 migration for sales support to allow concentration on UAT and unblocking remaining requirements. Open items remain clarifications from Mohan and Valerie on multiple stories.

action_items.text: Participant 1 will raise an escalation if required answers are not received by tomorrow so items can be addressed before the Tuesday grooming session (13:36)
Participant 2 will handle waiting agent-force stories directly with Harish or Valerie rather than having Participant 1 chase those items (14:52)
Participant 1 will start sending regular reports to Matt (16:10)
Participant 1 will sit with Drashti to migrate existing requirements into the Canvas format (20:15)
Participant 2 will send the Canvas requirements template and introduce it to the team on the next F&E call (22:25)
Participant 1 will reach out to Venkat to request questions for v3 and required information (23:21)

key_questions: Do you need help getting the business to provide requirements faster now that you have new contractors? (07:05)
Are we part of Wave 1 for the linear migration? (12:31)
Can the team use the Canvas requirements template and migrate current requirements into it? (22:17)

title: 29/04/2026 - Ponta a Ponta Release 1 com o Negocio
report_link: Ponta a Ponta Release 1 com o Negocio
summary:
A reunião teve como objetivo demonstrar e alinhar o fluxo de criação, preenchimento e versionamento de catálogos de preço (pricebooks), incluindo entradas por produto (PBE), governança por tarefas/filas e regras de ativação. A apresentação mostrou como criar um modelo de venda por produto, inserir PBEs manualmente ou via carga em planilha, e que clonar um produto simplifica o processo ao trazer dados já preenchidos. Foi explicado que o PBE é uma página dentro do livro de preços e que uma entrada só pode ser ativada se vinculada a um produto ativo, com um guard-rail que impede ativação sem cadastro completo e SKU de faturamento.
Em um exemplo prático com o produto "Farmácia Jacobina" definiu-se modelo de venda padrão, preço de lista e a criação de uma tarefa que dispara para a fila de pricing; Marcela foi apontada para preencher os dados enquanto Túlio (financeiro) ficará responsável por gerar o SKU retroativamente. O time demonstrou preenchimentos dependentes do produto-pai (incentivos, bolsa, matrículas, prazos), a configuração de validade por SKAL, o fluxo de conclusão de tarefas em grupo com transição entre G1 e G2, e o registro de histórico/versionamento. Foi solicitada uma demo em QA do histórico carimbado e alinhada a validação com a outra equipe (Stargate) e a liberação de acessos em produção.

action_items.text: Kilton: Adicionar no backlog pessoal a criação de um fluxo para preencher automaticamente o modelo de venda (03:23)
Túlio: Gerar os SKUs no CEA financeiro e, após geração, inserir os SKUs no sistema para vincular ao produto (10:07)
Marcela: Atualizar as informações do curso e preencher os campos do produto conforme a tarefa criada para concluir a primeira etapa até o dia 7 (13:53)
Cleilson: Preparar uma demo em QA que mostre duas versões no produto e o histórico carimbado das atualizações (19:29)
Participant 1: Agendar nova sessão de demonstração se o time solicitar (36:10)
Participant 5: Validar com a equipe externa (Stargate) e pedir o acesso dos participantes (36:49)
Participante: Liberar os acessos em produção para o time revisar o ambiente em produção (37:03)

key_questions: Por que não automatizar o preenchimento do modelo de venda ao criar o produto, já que ao clonar isso vem carregado? (02:23)
Qual é o preço a ser colocado para a Farmácia Jacobina? (10:39)
É possível demonstrar agora como ficaria a tela de histórico carimbado com múltiplas versões? (17:38)
O prazo de validade da cotação é definido de forma geral ou por SKAL? (22:59)
Conseguimos passar por todo o conteúdo ou será necessária mais uma sessão? (36:01)
blockers: Em um exemplo prático com o produto "Farmácia Jacobina" definiu-se modelo de venda padrão, preço de lista e a criação de uma tarefa que dispara para a fila de pricing; Marcela foi apontada para preencher os dados enquanto Túlio (financeiro) ficará responsável por gerar o SKU retroativamente. O time demonstrou preenchimentos dependentes do produto-pai (incentivos, bolsa, matrículas, prazos), a configuração de validade por SKAL, o fluxo de conclusão de tarefas em grupo com transição entre G1 e G2, e o registro de histórico/versionamento. Foi solicitada uma demo em QA do histórico carimbado e alinhada a validação com a outra equipe (Stargate) e a liberação de acessos em produção.

title: 29/04/2026 - Sprint Backlog Readiness
summary:
The meeting reviewed sprint readiness and onboarding ramp for Kilton using the Linear tool, focusing on ensuring the team — including new contractors — has enough prepared work to stay productive. The discussion began with the current sprint status and availability; participants noted contractors are productive but need at least two sprints of prepared backlog to avoid idle time. Kilton described documenting reasons stories are blocked and working to unblock items ahead of planning. Mohan recommended stronger grooming practices, capturing capacity before planning, and using a Kanban board for specific workstreams such as agent deployments.
The team agreed to tighten planning and definition-of-ready practices, require acceptance criteria on all stories, and use planning poker to reduce mid-sprint clarifications. They decided sprint demos will occur on the last Friday of each sprint, extend the demo slot by 15 minutes, and ask presenters to prepare three-minute recordings to play during the meeting. Mohan volunteered to take ownership of the story to remove historical cases from agent responses and to update that story’s acceptance criteria to expedite delivery.

action_items.text: Kilton will explore Linear to confirm whether Kanban boards are available and whether to set up one workstream as Kanban and another as Agile (08:18)
Kilton will continue documenting waiting reasons on backlog stories and actively work to unblock those stories prior to the next planning session (16:18)
Kilton will schedule sprint demos on the last Friday of the sprint, extend the demo slot by 15 minutes, and message the team to prepare three-minute recordings (19:42)
Mohan will update the acceptance criteria and communicate with Venkat and Anu to remove historical cases from agent responses (24:33)

key_questions: Is there anyone on the team without work right now? (04:06)
Is Kanban available in Linear and should we consider using it for agent workstreams? (07:51)
How comfortable are you with functional business knowledge across processes and can you support UAT? (25:21)
blockers: The meeting reviewed sprint readiness and onboarding ramp for Kilton using the Linear tool, focusing on ensuring the team — including new contractors — has enough prepared work to stay productive. The discussion began with the current sprint status and availability; participants noted contractors are productive but need at least two sprints of prepared backlog to avoid idle time. Kilton described documenting reasons stories are blocked and working to unblock items ahead of planning. Mohan recommended stronger grooming practices, capturing capacity before planning, and using a Kanban board for specific workstreams such as agent deployments.

title: 29/04/2026 - Sync with Val
summary: The meeting reviewed recent software installation, access issues, and work to align feature parity between legal record controls and commission record types. Participant 1 reported installing the software and handing it to a colleague, but noted confusion about that user's configuration that needs clarification; Participant 2 walked through a support ticket, confirmed a link Dave can use to check DevTap access, and verified that Dave has appropriate DevTap permissions. The group reviewed a Basecamp migration where developer Kat confirmed she already has required access and will add a project shortcut, and Participant 1 asked whether others want to be involved in the baby-scan–specific work. Participant 1 reported that the missing buttons for the legal workflow have been completed and that the same button behavior must be implemented for commissions; Denka was identified as the commission contact and Jess as the owner for packaging and prioritization. title: 29/04/2026 - Rescheduling and Closing Remarks
summary: The recording focused on rescheduling a meeting to accommodate Valerie and Veronica and on closing remarks from Kilton Fernandes. title: 29/04/2026 - DET DSU Meeting.mp4
summary:
The meeting reviewed sprint progress, testing status, and access/integration work across several ongoing development and QA items. Adesh reported pipeline work and that he temporarily disabled over 200 lint violations to verify the pipeline runs. Kalayvani described completed changes to the organization ID field for the Clover Plus case, has a second PR awaiting review and answers from Anno, and offered to pick up an unassigned story after finishing current tasks. Several stories were moved to "waiting" with reasons to be added to the board.
Testing and access items followed: Anu and others continue GoCloud Plus case testing with outstanding scenarios yet to validate, and Anita reported automation and setup blockers plus a one-point story to validate a missing-button behavior from the prior sprint. Dina raised whether the approval-process fix should be specific or global; that question remained unresolved. Venkat and Amy raised Basecamp access and integration ownership, and responsibility to contact the Basecamp team for build access was assigned.

action_items.text: Speaker 1 will start the DSU and share their screen for the session (03:17)
Adesh will post his pipeline doubts and questions on the team Slack channel (05:12)
Kalayvani will take the currently unassigned story after completing her two active stories (07:00)
Speaker 1 will put Amy and the team in contact with the Basecamp team to enable DO 6.2/EMP DO integration (13:30)
Speaker 1 will message Drashti and confirm whether the approval fix must be global, and will follow up by tomorrow or the next business day (17:04)
Venkat will reach out to the Basecamp team to request access to run the GenFins build (19:28)
Conference room participant (Speaker 5) will create a separate Slack channel and test the validation rule by deactivating it and setting debug logs as needed (23:54)

key_questions: Should the fix for the approval behavior be applied only to the specific approval process referenced in the attached document or should it be applied globally across all approval processes? (14:34)
Why is a PSR deletion not triggering after a record-type change and how is the validation rule affecting status changes during that process? (20:35)

title: 29/04/2026 - DET Grooming Session.mp4
summary: The meeting was a grooming session to review new backlog items, confirm blockers on waiting stories, and size selected work. The team opened by restricting the agenda to new stories and noting that waiting items would retain documented reasons pending follow-up. The RevRack/Omni-channel capacity story was presented and discussed in detail: the change aims to prevent manually assigned non-RevRack cases from consuming Omni capacity, the technical trigger points were reviewed, and planning poker produced a median estimate of five. A contract modification automation story was identified as overlapping with another team's work and was moved to waiting pending confirmation from Drashti and Rusty and verification of a May 22 deployment dependency. An agent-response story to exclude historical case data from AI replies was accepted by a developer who requested sample records and live examples to clarify triggers and data sources. The team also reviewed a KMOD auto-approval restriction for ISV reseller cases, confirmed intended behavior for routing to manual review based on reseller contract types or price book, estimated the story at three, and moved it to triage. Kilton committed to attaching existing documentation to the ticket and to tracking and unblocking waiting items raised in the discussion.

action_items.text: Speaker 2 will follow up with Thomas to request the missing information for the waiting ticket referenced earlier. (02:59)
Speaker 2 will triage the RevRack story, add the requested reference information, and set its status in the backlog by today or before next week's planning. (17:35)
Venkat (or the session organizer) will clear or restart the planning‑poker session to resolve refresh/organizer issues and allow future sizing rounds. (18:33)
Speaker 2 will confirm with Drashti/Rusty whether the contract modification automation overlaps existing work and will move the ticket to waiting or backlog based on that confirmation. (21:02)
Speaker 1 will take ownership of the agent historical‑data exclusion story and will obtain sample records, session data, and live examples to clarify triggers and requirements. (23:20)
Kilton will follow up with the stakeholder who raised the requirement and add any missing items to the waiting queue (25:38)
Kilton will attach the documentation he has for the KMOD auto-approval story to the story ticket (27:35)
Kilton will prepare and show the Cayman/Salesforce view for the stories to help the team understand the process (27:46)
Kilton will add the estimated value of three to the story and move it to triage (31:02)
Kilton will work on unblocking the waiting stories and resolve the dependencies identified in the DSU meeting (31:55)

key_questions: Is the ticket with no description a new item or missing information? (04:36)
How should developers vote in planning poker—estimate dev effort only or whole story? (12:32)
Who owns the contract modification/process change and has it been developed by another team? (20:43)
Are there sample records or session data showing the agent retrieving case history for replies? (23:20)
Can Kilton show the Cayman process or Salesforce view for the stories to help the team grasp them more easily? (27:46)
blockers: The meeting was a grooming session to review new backlog items, confirm blockers on waiting stories, and size selected work. The team opened by restricting the agenda to new stories and noting that waiting items would retain documented reasons pending follow-up. The RevRack/Omni-channel capacity story was presented and discussed in detail: the change aims to prevent manually assigned non-RevRack cases from consuming Omni capacity, the technical trigger points were reviewed, and planning poker produced a median estimate of five. A contract modification automation story was identified as overlapping with another team's work and was moved to waiting pending confirmation from Drashti and Rusty and verification of a May 22 deployment dependency. An agent-response story to exclude historical case data from AI replies was accepted by a developer who requested sample records and live examples to clarify triggers and data sources. The team also reviewed a KMOD auto-approval restriction for ISV reseller cases, confirmed intended behavior for routing to manual review based on reseller contract types or price book, estimated the story at three, and moved it to triage. Kilton committed to attaching existing documentation to the ticket and to tracking and unblocking waiting items raised in the discussion.

title: 29/04/2026 - Daily
summary:
A reunião teve como objetivo alinhar progresso nas entregas, validações em QA e preparação para deploys em produção. A equipe atualizou o estado da integração com o WebSocket e deploys já realizados por Fabrício; ele documentou credenciais de desenvolvimento e pediu que André e Fabiano substituam por credenciais de produção quando adequado, enquanto valida permissões e planeja testes em produção após a criação de filas SQS e demais recursos. Clayson informou avanços na vacinação do portfólio, ajuste de timestamp e está preparando um relatório sobre permissões para mover o relacionamento do portfólio para QA e parcial, com validação prevista segundo os testes da Adriana.
Houve alinhamento sobre priorização do backlog e compromisso de fatiar e escrever histórias após o almoço para organizar as iterações 15 e 16, visando PRD em 15 de maio. O time combinou validações em QA com Adriana e outros perfis para confirmar 100% antes da homologação de negócio e tentou aproveitar o refinamento de segunda-feira para alocar homologação. Foram também definidas tarefas de validação de componentes e uma reatribuição/bloqueio de tarefas por um desenvolvedor até 12:30. Encerrou-se com confiança mútua, menção ao Paradoxo de Stockdale para explicar esperança versus otimismo na equipe, e registro de uma lista de atividades pendentes e coordenação informal pós-almoço.

action_items.text: André: Alterar as credenciais de desenvolvimento para credenciais de produção conforme a documentação deixada por Fabrício (01:29)
Fabiano: Alterar as credenciais de desenvolvimento para credenciais de produção conforme a documentação deixada por Fabrício (01:29)
Participant 3: Validar permissões, campos e classes e testar em produção assim que as filas SQS e recursos estiverem criados (03:20)
Participant 1: Realizar a validação inicial em QA e gravar dois testes (um individual e outro com Adriana) para confirmar 100% hoje (09:59)
Participant 3: Revisar componentes enviados até aproximadamente 12:30 e iniciar a reatribuição, devolução e bloqueio de tarefas (11:34)
Participant 2: Colocar o perfil de administrador para Adriana em produção antes da validação em produção, mediante aviso (12:50)
Participant 2: Marcar reunião no final do dia com Participant 1 e Adriana para revisar o documento e agendar testes/homologação (13:27)
Participant 5: Explique o Paradoxo de Stockdale ao grupo em próxima discussão (14:41)
Participant 1: Trazer explicação correta do Paradoxo na próxima reunião (15:57)

blockers: Houve alinhamento sobre priorização do backlog e compromisso de fatiar e escrever histórias após o almoço para organizar as iterações 15 e 16, visando PRD em 15 de maio. O time combinou validações em QA com Adriana e outros perfis para confirmar 100% antes da homologação de negócio e tentou aproveitar o refinamento de segunda-feira para alocar homologação. Foram também definidas tarefas de validação de componentes e uma reatribuição/bloqueio de tarefas por um desenvolvedor até 12:30. Encerrou-se com confiança mútua, menção ao Paradoxo de Stockdale para explicar esperança versus otimismo na equipe, e registro de uma lista de atividades pendentes e coordenação informal pós-almoço.

title: 29/04/2026 - Alinhamento Interno Acessos e Perfis
summary:
A reunião teve como objetivo organizar o recorte da sprint 15 para 16, detalhar o fluxo de criação de catálogos e definir responsabilidades e permissões para gestão do portfólio. Inicialmente foram alinhadas ações de sprint: fechar a 15, preparar o recorte para a 16 e escrever histórias para manter o time produtivo, com pedido explícito para cobrança sobre resposta pendente ao Dre; também foi solicitado incluir Matheus na lista de acessos ao catálogo e avaliada a opção de usar o ambiente de UAT para testes finais.
Em seguida o time mapeou o fluxo técnico: criar catálogo, vincular categorias, atribuir produtos e gerar entradas do Price Book (PBE) com validade e preço de lista; o gestor de portfólio deve criar e vincular catálogos/categorias, o agente de pricing tende a configurar PBE e preencher prazo e preço de lista, e o financeiro deve preencher SKU nas entradas. Foi discutida a granularidade de permissões (inclusive por campo para preço) e identificados os perfis necessários (NAPs Portfólio, NAPs Contratos, NAPs Operações). O processo operacional definido inclui gerar a planilha inicial, enviar V1 aos NAPs, e abrir tarefas para regulatório, planejamento acadêmico, pricing e operações (com regulatório fora do esforço imediato). A equipe testou a interface de abertura/conclusão de tarefas, validou atributos como status G2 e edição em lote, e acordou produzir um documento com vídeo das etapas; Clayson enviará a relação de perfis e Dri fará os testes após o envio do material.

action_items.text: Participant 1: Escrever as histórias e realizar o recorte da sprint 15 para a 16 (06:59)
Dre: Cobrar o participante principal pela resposta sobre o recorte e as histórias (07:58)
Participant 1: Enviar a lista de acessos do Matheus (08:47)
Participant 1: Criar o catálogo de gestão de portfólio (09:26)
Cleuson: Investigar o papel chamado gestor de portfólio (09:41)
Participant 1: Comparar o ambiente de UAT e sugerir qual ambiente usar para testes (09:46)
Time de Pricing: Decidir se será necessário quebrar permissões para quem pode editar/configurar catálogos de preço e comunicar a decisão (18:12)
Matheus: Confirmar se o gestor de portfólio é o responsável pela criação manual de produtos ou se existe outro responsável (22:37)
Financeiro: Preencher os SKUs nas entradas do catálogo de preço (PBE) conforme definido no mapeamento (24:37)
Larissa: Enviar as tarefas para os times indicados no fluxo do portfólio (32:52)
Participante 1: Gravar a sessão, criar um documento com o vídeo das etapas e enviar para Dri (52:52)
Clayson: Enviar a relação de perfis e quem executa cada etapa para que o documento seja montado (52:57)
Dri: Realizar os testes do fluxo após receber o documento e o vídeo das etapas (53:13)

key_questions: Quando os testes serão iniciados e quais são os testes a serem executados? (06:39)
Quem deve ter acesso ao catálogo? (08:42)
Você prefere que comparemos o ambiente de UAT para os testes finais? (09:46)
O catálogo de preços (PB) é responsabilidade do gestor de portfólio também? (18:08)
Os produtos vão ser criados em batch ou manualmente? (25:16)
Há alguém do time acadêmico disponível para atuar no fluxo? (38:05)
Há alguém de operações comerciais presente para receber tarefas? (39:08)

title: 29/04/2026 - Checkpoint
summary: A gravação registra uma breve reunião operacional focada em organização de agendas e alocação de participantes para validações e apurações de atividades ao longo do dia e dias seguintes. Kilton Fernandes informou que a equipe de São Paulo fará avaliações e apurações para o dia, tarde, noite e dias subsequentes, e mencionou dificuldades na tabulação do processo com o autor envolvido. Houve checagens de áudio e dificuldades técnicas, com Kilton e outros confirmando problemas de som e reconexão antes de estabilizar a chamada. Kilton comunicou que já havia orientado os meninos para que ao menos um participe da agenda prevista para mais tarde e reforçou que haverá uma primeira validação do acadêmico com foco também no consumo para fechar o ciclo. Por fim, Kilton informou que precisava sair para outra reunião técnica e se despediu após confirmar que não havia pontos pendentes a tratar com ele.

action_items.text: São Paulo: Realizar as avaliações e apurações para o dia, tarde, noite e os dias subsequentes conforme indicado (00:14)
Kilton Fernandes: Participar/ir à reunião sobre perfis de acesso com Adri e Matheus conforme mencionado (01:37)
Kilton Fernandes: Garantir que pelo menos um dos meninos participe da agenda de hoje (02:33)

key_questions: ## 💬 Chapters & Topics

title: 28/04/2026 - Linear Basics_ AMER_EMEA Friendly  pt 1
report_link: Linear Basics_ AMER_EMEA Friendly  Google Chrome.mp4
summary:
The meeting reviewed the DET team's adoption of Linear as the centralized workspace for intake, planning, execution, and portfolio reporting, outlining the three-layer model of initiatives, projects, and issues and how those map to existing GUS/Salesforce terminology. The presenters demonstrated navigation, team hierarchy, triage workflows, Slack intake options (forms and the Linear Slack agent), automated coding agents that create pull requests, GitHub sync automations that update issue status and enable in-app reviews, and views including Kanban, list, burn-up charts, cycle time dashboards, and Gantt-style initiative timelines. Project-level features shown included properties, milestones, cross-team assignments, labels (including Salesforce guardrails), milestone burn-ups, and project dashboards with owners and target dates.
The group covered onboarding and support: a Wave 1 canvas, a DET Linear Onboarding Support Channel, a playbook and FAQs, and named customer success and engineering contacts. Integration constraints and decisions were clarified: every Linear team must have a corresponding GUS entry to avoid pipeline failures, GUS will become historical after the migration deadline at the end of Q2, a security-bug-and-dependency sync is blocked with a workaround in progress, there is no current plan to auto-route P360 intake, and licenses currently cover only DET personnel and business partners lack direct license coverage. Open questions about routing, Slack/Okta rollout, and tagging requirements remain.

action_items.text: Share will open incoming issues in triage and provide shape to them (05:24)
Team leads will clean and prepare their GUS backlogs for migration following the provided guidelines and the Wave 1 pre-onboarding checklist before the Monday go-live (51:20)
Shilpa will schedule persona-based trainings (TPM, portfolio managers, and other personas) and add them to participants' calendars starting next week (57:28)
The program core team will pre-assign Wave 1 teams based on the data submitted by participants (01:00:48)
Shilpa will ensure Okta SSO is completed so users receive the Linear login email and have access to pinned enablement materials (01:01:51)
Shilpa will send login instructions and guidance for the Linear production instance on May 4th via the Slack channel (01:02:02)
Craig and the Linear team will add team-level Slack integration guidance to the playbook and share specifics shortly (01:03:55)
Teams will notify the Linear team of specific calculation needs and usage feedback as they start using the tool (01:07:33)
Each team will work with the integration team to create a corresponding GUS team for their Linear team to prevent EP pipeline failures (01:08:11)
All teams will complete transition from GUS to Linear by the end of Q2 (01:08:58)
Shilpa will work through a workaround to resolve the blocked security-bug and dependency integration between GUS and Linear (01:09:25)
Teams will use Slack workflows and custom channels to surface business intake into their Linear backlogs while business licenses are unavailable (01:11:21)
Participants will provide feedback and requests for customizations to the presenters and PDO resources during onboarding (01:12:03)

key_questions: Can users search for work items (issues) across projects or other teams? (56:45)
When will persona-based trainings for TPMs and portfolio managers be scheduled? (57:17)
Do all issues need to be tagged to a project or initiative, and is the hierarchy mandatory? (57:55)
Can innovation work or a "POC" be added in Linear, or should only committed/prioritized work be included? (58:24)
Is it possible to set up a trigger for a team to conduct reviews or schedule periodic reviews in Linear? (59:16)
Can teams create sub-teams for squads, or should they operate within a single team? (01:00:22)
How are retrospectives handled and captured in Linear? (01:02:41)
When will the Linear Slack app/integration be available? (01:03:25)
Can issues in triage be converted into projects if warranted? (01:05:32)
How is enterprise pipeline accounting handling org and code ownership and fast lane deployments? (01:07:39)
Is there a cutoff date for when work should move from GUS to Linear and should teams keep creating user stories in GUS until told otherwise? (01:08:49)
Does Linear have a standardized intake process and can requirements be routed from the P360 tracker into Slack or Linear? (01:09:49)
Will there be exceptions or special handling (e.g., licenses) for NQE high-priority program teams with tight timelines? (01:10:48)
blockers: The group covered onboarding and support: a Wave 1 canvas, a DET Linear Onboarding Support Channel, a playbook and FAQs, and named customer success and engineering contacts. Integration constraints and decisions were clarified: every Linear team must have a corresponding GUS entry to avoid pipeline failures, GUS will become historical after the migration deadline at the end of Q2, a security-bug-and-dependency sync is blocked with a workaround in progress, there is no current plan to auto-route P360 intake, and licenses currently cover only DET personnel and business partners lack direct license coverage. Open questions about routing, Slack/Okta rollout, and tagging requirements remain.

title: 24/04/2026 - DET Sprint 4B Planning.mp4
summary:
The meeting reviewed Sprint 4A closed and rolling stories to prioritize work and assign items for Sprint 4B. The team validated rollovers including GovCloud case approval and logic optimization, confirmed product support and data story statuses, and pushed a scalability logging task forward after insufficient time. In-progress follow-ups were identified for channel bot updates, sales report data-cloud migration blocked by data-cloud bugs, sandbox refresh work split between QA and dev, and onboarding documentation already noted in Quip.
During backlog triage the group confirmed sizes, owners, and QA responsibilities for multiple stories and agreed to calculate sprint capacity before finalizing assignments because of a public holiday and planned time off. Several stories were identified as externally blocked by picklist cleanup, a production freeze, or awaiting business examples; the team decided to move fully blocked items to backlog or waiting and to document reasons in Chatter. Technical topics discussed included removing an incorrect Omni queue validation rule, debugging agent/Udesk utterance collisions, and a spike on Slack permission/visibility tied to Org62. The team evaluated an email-to-case test approach using a shared Gmail box and noted provisioning dependencies. Venkat agreed to take a metadata migration ask and consult Harish for complexity and pointing, Anu was suggested for QA, an Agent Force item was rolled over, a five-point QE automation story was created for Anitha, acceptance criteria and technical debt stories were assigned, and a DET Sales Engineering Standards check remained pending with Val.

Several stories were identified as externally blocked by picklist cleanup, a production freeze, or awaiting business examples; the team decided to move fully blocked items to backlog or waiting and to document reasons in Chatter. Technical topics discussed included removing an incorrect Omni queue validation rule, debugging agent/Udesk utterance collisions, and a spike on Slack permission/visibility tied to Org62. The team evaluated an email-to-case test approach using a shared Gmail box and noted provisioning dependencies. Venkat agreed to take a metadata migration ask and consult Harish for complexity and pointing, Anu was suggested for QA, an Agent Force item was rolled over, a five-point QE automation story was created for Anitha, acceptance criteria and technical debt stories were assigned, and a DET Sales Engineering Standards check remained pending with Val.
